summary: Phase 4 verification COMPLETE. New WhatsApp Tech Provider backend endpoints and Chatwoot-removal frontend changes all pass. Backend: 54/54 passed, 1 skipped (Chatwoot SSO — known upstream config issue per request, ignored). Frontend: client dashboard hides ALL Chatwoot references, shows company_name 'متجر أحمد' in both required places (header badge + greeting pill), and the Channels page successfully calls the NEW POST /api/whatsapp/connect endpoint (verified via network capture, not the legacy /me/channels/whatsapp). Mock provisioning works end-to-end.

minor.issue: Returns HTTPException JSON {detail:'verify_token_mismatch'} on 403. Meta's verifier expects only the challenge or 403 body; FastAPI's default JSON body is acceptable but technically Meta ignores it. Behavior is correct: empty/unmatched token => 403, matched => echoes hub.challenge.
When META not configured, accepts ALL events without signature verification (by design, but be aware once META_APP_SECRET is set the gate becomes active and unsigned requests will be rejected).
MOCK only — no signature verification (pre-existing, intentional per earlier requests).

action_items: (Pre-existing, NOT for this iteration) Chatwoot upstream CHATWOOT_URL=https://letsm.io returns 404 for /platform/api/v1/accounts. Per request, this was skipped in this iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: whatsapp_meta.public_config_for_frontend(): correctly exposes only {enabled, app_id, config_id, graph_version}. Secret/token never leaks (verified). Good.

verify_signature(): correctly returns False when app_secret is empty, but the route at /webhooks/whatsapp gates with `if is_configured() and not verify_signature(...)`. This means when META is unconfigured the webhook silently accepts ANY body — fine for now but ensure ops team sets WHATSAPP_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN and META_APP_SECRET before flipping the app to live on Meta.

GET /webhooks/whatsapp returns int(challenge) which FastAPI serializes as JSON number; Meta expects plain text challenge body. Recommend wrapping with PlainTextResponse(challenge, status_code=200) to be strictly Meta-compliant — currently works in dev but may fail Meta's verification webhook handshake in production.
server.py is now 913 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend extracting /webhooks/* and /whatsapp/* into a routers/whatsapp.py module.

Channels.jsx: disconnect uses legacy DELETE /me/channels/whatsapp while connect uses POST /whatsapp/connect — asymmetric. Consider adding DELETE /whatsapp/disconnect for consistency, or document the asymmetry.
DashboardLayout/Dashboard: company_name pill correctly hidden when empty (verified). Good defensive UI.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase4_whatsapp.py (new — 18 test cases covering /whatsapp/config, /whatsapp/connect mock+idempotency, webhook verify handshake, webhook event ingestion, legacy /me/channels/whatsapp regression, /auth/me + company_name presence, /me/account/wallet/topup regression, admin overview)
test_credentials: Admin: admin@socialhub.om / Admin@2026. Client: ahmed@test.com / Test@1234 (company_name='متجر أحمد').
seed_data_creation: Used the pre-seeded ahmed@test.com client for UI tests. Backend pytest registers a unique test_phase4_<uuid>@test.com for each module run; no permanent data created. /api/whatsapp/connect upsert ensures only one WA channel per user (verified via idempotency test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run all backend tests with: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://storage-system-test.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/results.xml`. Phase 4 specific file: test_phase4_whatsapp.py. Frontend verified flows: client login → dashboard (company_name visible × 2, NO Chatwoot anywhere), Channels disconnect+reconnect (network capture confirmed POST /api/whatsapp/connect is hit, NOT legacy endpoint), Billing & Wallet pages load clean. The Chatwoot SSO skip in test_phase3_flows.py is intentional and unchanged.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in this iteration.
